name: Compression garments
description: Flat-knit compression wear on lipedema limbs — worn through waking hours. Class II (20-30 mmHg) typical; custom fit preferred over off-the-shelf.
rationale: Counteracts interstitial fluid buildup; reduces pain and swelling; slows progression of tissue changes.

name: Manual Lymphatic Drainage (MLD)
description: Specialised gentle-pressure massage following lymphatic pathways. Practitioner session weekly; self-MLD daily if trained.
rationale: Mechanically moves lymph past obstructed/insufficient drainage pathways; reduces pain and fluid retention.

name: Dry skin brushing
description: Soft natural-bristle brush in lymphatic directions (toward heart) for 3-5 minutes before shower.
rationale: Cheap daily adjunct to MLD; stimulates superficial lymphatic flow.

name: Swimming / water walking
description: Pool-based movement 2-3x/week. Water pressure acts as whole-body compression.
rationale: Low-impact; hydrostatic pressure aids lymphatic return; avoids inflammation from impact.

name: Walking
description: 30-60 min daily at conversational pace. Break into short walks if needed.
rationale: Calf muscle pump drives venous/lymphatic return; daily consistent low dose > occasional intensity.

name: Rebounding (mini-trampoline)
description: 5-15 min gentle bouncing, feet may stay in contact with mat. Not jumping.
rationale: Rhythmic gravity shifts strongly pump lymphatics; low-impact if gentle.

name: Cycling (stationary preferred)
description: Low-resistance cycling; stationary bike minimizes ankle/knee impact.
rationale: Leg pump without ground impact; good option when walking aggravates.

name: Avoid high-impact exercise
description: Skip running, HIIT jumping, heavy plyometrics on affected limbs.
rationale: High impact aggravates inflamed adipose and lymphatic tissue; worsens symptoms in most patients.

name: Sleep hygiene
description: 7-9 hours consistent schedule. Cool dark room. No screens for 1 hour before bed. Elevate legs if swelling overnight.
rationale: Lymphatic clearance via glymphatic system peaks during deep sleep; insufficient sleep increases systemic inflammation.

name: Leg elevation
description: Elevate legs 15-30 cm during sleep or rest periods — wedge pillow or bed risers at foot end.
rationale: Gravity assists venous and lymphatic return overnight; reduces morning swelling.

name: Stress management practice
description: Choose one: meditation, breathwork, yoga, journaling. 10-20 min daily consistency over intensity.
rationale: Chronic cortisol elevation drives inflammation, fluid retention, and insulin dysregulation — all RAD symptom amplifiers.

name: Diaphragmatic / vagal breathing
description: 5-10 min slow 4-7-8 or box breathing. Can pair with MLD or leg elevation.
rationale: Activates parasympathetic state; deep diaphragm movement also directly massages thoracic duct (main lymphatic return).
